content.description:
The REPL is a proper item with its own view, using a panel shell for the overlay UX.

## Architecture (Approach B — implemented March 2026)

**Panel shell** — [repl-ui](item://f1111111-0003-0000-0000-000000000000) manages the collapsible bottom panel chrome (collapse bar, height splitter, Ctrl+\\ toggle). It renders the REPL view into its content area by calling `viewModule.render()` directly.

**View** — [repl-view](item://f1111111-0003-0002-0000-000000000000) renders the input textarea, output transcript, vertical splitter, and action buttons. Handles code execution (JS and Hob), console interception, history navigation.

**Item** — [REPL](item://f1111111-0003-0003-0000-000000000000) singleton instance of type [repl-session](item://f1111111-0003-0001-0000-000000000000). Stores `content.history` (last 50 commands) and `content.transcript` (last 50 code+result entries, persisted with debounce). `content.inputWidthPercent` stores the vertical splitter position.

## Why This Shape

- History and transcript persist across reloads
- The REPL is inspectable, exportable, and editable like any item
- The panel overlay UX is preserved — Ctrl+\\ still works everywhere
- The view can also render standalone if the REPL item is navigated to directly
- Future: when spatial canvas gets dock-collapse, the panel shell can be retired and the REPL becomes a normal docked canvas item

## Recovery

- Safe mode (`?safe=1`) skips all user code
- Browser console: `window.kernel` and the full API are always accessible
- The panel shell gracefully shows an error if the view fails to load
